name: Mathewrean Otieno Magundho
tagline: Cybersecurity & IT Operations | CTF Competitor & Mentor | Technical Writer | Student Leader
eyebrow: Cybersecurity • Digital Forensics • Network Security • Research
summary: Computer Security and Digital Forensics student at JOOUST focused on digital security operations while serving as SIIS Congressman to bridge technical security with student leadership; actively seeking an IT Operations or Cybersecurity attachment to deploy blue-team and incident response skills.
about: I’m a Cybersecurity & Forensics student who learns by doing. As a CTFZone Campus Ambassador (handle: M47H3WR34N), I hunt vulnerabilities and master defensive playbooks, treating each challenge as an opportunity to sharpen my technical edge and understand the "why" behind every security control. Off the terminal, I translate complex concepts into lighter, community-ready insight as a technical writer for JOOUST ISACA. That same drive for problem-solving led me to co-develop Rechiro for the Western Region Research Hackathon, where we deployed digital innovation to bridge supply-chain gaps. I’m stacking certifications from Cybershujaa, Cisco Netacad and OPSWAT Academy to stay ahead of fast-moving threats, and I’m now seeking a forward-thinking team where I can channel my energy into securing digital ecosystems while learning from experienced practitioners.
